mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.09% signals an extended period of monetary accommodation. As long as FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%, the low-rate regime sustains downward pressure on long-term Treasury yields through portfolio rebalancing and carry-trade dynamics, keeping 10-year yields suppressed over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary accommodation regime with near-zero rates
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 15 basis points on average in instances where FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window during low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 6.3% signals labor market slack. When UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, persistent slack dampens wage growth expectations and inflation expectations, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to decline as real rate expectations compress and inflation risk premium shrinks over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: slack labor market with elevated unemployment
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 10 basis points on average when UNRATE > 6.0%, or the correlation between UNRATE and 10-year yield direction is positive (> 0.1) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 437,648 million and housing starts (HOUST) at 1,072 thousand jointly signal robust consumer demand and construction activity. When both RSAFS and HOUST grow month-over-month, aggregate demand pressures build, forcing the 10-year Treasury yield upward as growth expectations and inflation expectations rise over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 2.0%
regime_hint: late-cycle demand expansion with synchronized consumption and housing strength
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 10 basis points on average when both RSAFS and HOUST growth conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 102.74 indicates near-trend supply capacity utilization. When INDPRO declines month-over-month by more than 0.3%, it signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as inflation expectations increase despite growth concerns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 15 basis points on average when INDPRO falls > 0.3%, or the direction of yield change is negative in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 138,463 thousand and unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 6.3% jointly indicate a tightening labor market when PAYEMS grows month-over-month and UNRATE declines simultaneously. This dual signal predicts accelerating wage growth and inflation, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.2% AND UNRATE month-over-month decline > 0.1pp
regime_hint: tight labor market with synchronized employment gains and unemployment decline
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 12 basis points on average when both labor tightening conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 236.254 and federal funds rate at 0.09% create a deeply negative real rate environment. When the real rate (FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPI inflation) falls below -2.0%, it signals monetary policy is highly accommodative, suppressing long-term Treasury yields as investors flee to real assets over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: deeply negative real rate regime with monetary accommodation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 25 basis points on average when real rates are below -2.0%, or the yield-real rate correlation is positive (> 0.15) over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 15,902.9 billion (chained 2017 dollars) and retail sales (RSAFS) at 437,648 million jointly measure aggregate demand momentum. When GDPC1 growth (quarter-over-quarter annualized) exceeds 2.5% and RSAFS shows sustained month-over-month growth > 0.3%, the economy is in late-cycle expansion, pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward as growth and inflation expectations rise over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2.5% AND RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.3% per month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized GDP and consumption strength
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 20 basis points on average when both demand conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 102.74 and consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 236.254 jointly signal supply-demand balance. When INDPRO growth slows to below 0.2% month-over-month while CPIAUCSL accelerates above its 12-month trend, supply constraints are binding and inflation expectations rise, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next 5 trading days as a leading indicator of stagflation risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0.2% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth > 0.3%
regime_hint: stagflation risk regime with supply constraints and inflation acceleration
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 8 basis points on average when both supply-inflation conditions are met, or the direction of yield change is negative in > 50% of triggered instances over any 12-month window.
